gloss: to put, place; to dedicate, make (sacred)

source_notes: thematic root present; apotropaic inscription verb ("let no villain strike me"); cf. PC *gweneti (OIr. gonaid "kills"), PIt *gʷʰen- (Lat. -fendō in dēfendō, offendō), PGmc noun only (*gundiz "battle"), PBS meaning-shifted (Lith. genù "drive")

source_notes: thematic root present (Ghandwa innovation — PIE present was nasal infix *ḱl̥néwti); everyday register for "hear, listen"; sacred counterpart (nasal infix form meaning "hearken, attend reverently") parked for future session; cf. PGmc *hauzijaną (jan-caus.), PBS *klausītei (iterative), PC *klinutor (nasal infix retained), PIt *kluēie- (stative) — all four sisters diverge

gloss: sacred fire-nature; the quality of being ensouled by fire
source_notes: PIE *h₁éngʷ-mn̥ ~ h₁n̥gʷ-ménes; *-mn̥ suffix forming noun of characteristic quality from the agnis root; nom/acc ~ gen ablaut (éng- ~ ang-) reflects full/zero-grade root alternation of the *-mn̥ paradigm
note: mass noun, no plural; 'the fire-nature of a thing', the condition of having Agnis within; cf. Sanskrit nā́man (name), ātman (self/breath) for the same suffix; cf. ógnos (#336) for ash/soot from same root *h₁engʷ- — both neuter n/men-stems via *-mn̥, both cosmologically central; the going-out of engman at death = death; gradient present in all things from Tier 1 upward; compressed and hidden in iron, near-surface in copper

gloss: dark, dusky (of a person or object)

Direct inherited from *mus-ko-; Germanic and Italic (EDL; EDPG).
Mountain from *mon- family; Italic and Celtic (EDL; EDPC). Distinct from kalnís (#114) which continues *ḱl̥H-ni-.
Naked family from *nogʷ-; derivatives differ across branches (EDL; EDPG).
Ship/vessel from *neh₂u-; Germanic and Italic (EDL; EDPG). No watercraft vocabulary currently in lexicon.
Short family from *mrgʰ-u-; u-stem adjective (EDL; EDPG).
Verb from *sed-; Celtic and Germanic (EDPC; EDPG). Cf. sessos (#329) for the participle. Swadesh core.
Smear/daub family from *h₂lei-; same root with different derivatives (EDL; EDPG). Concordance PG form garbled.
Spin family *(s)pen-; Germanic and Italic (EDL; EDPG). Concordance PIE form was truncated; root reconstructed.
Talk/speak from *tlokʷ-; Celtic-Italic isogloss (EDL; EDPC). Significant for western branch positioning.
Proximal demonstrative from s- family; Celtic and Italic (EDL; EDPC). Cf. tód (#274) for distal. Gaps file #8.
